title: Doria alata
intro: Doria alata, also known as Doria's wing or Winged Doria, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Malvaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Doria alata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Doria alata is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues.
